finding.summary: True peak is significantly higher than sample peak, indicating potential intersample peaks or very short transients that could cause clipping when summed with other tracks.
finding.probable_cause: High crest factor (12 dB) combined with a silence fraction of 0.38 suggests that the non‑silent portions are much louder than the averaged levels, and the true peak reaches -5.5 dBFS, leaving little headroom.
finding.confidence_reason: The 38% silence dilutes all level measurements; the actual signal levels during active portions may be even higher than reported, but we cannot be certain without segmenting the capture.

evidence_refs.description: True peak at -5.5 dBFS, very close to 0 dBFS.
Sample peak at -16 dBFS, much lower than true peak.
Crest factor of 12 dB indicates high dynamic range, typical of a pad but with potential for peaks.
38% silence suggests measured RMS and LUFS are diluted; true peak may be more representative of loudest moments.

proposal.reason: Reducing track volume by 6 dB provides 6 dB more headroom below 0 dBFS, lowering the true peak from -5.5 dBFS to approximately -11.5 dBFS, reducing clipping risk during mixdown. The track currently has no FX chain so volume is the only available adjustment.
